name: dispersed_repeat_alu
description: Alu Short Interspersed Nuclear Element (SINE): primate-specific ~300 bp dimeric element derived from 7SL RNA. With ~1.1 million copies it is the most abundant repeat in the human genome (~11% of total DNA). Subfamilies AluJ (oldest), AluS, AluY (youngest, still mobilising). GC-rich (50-65%) relative to surrounding DNA, with characteristic A-box and B-box internal RNA Pol III promoter elements. Shannon entropy near-genomic-average (~3.0-3.5) because Alus look 'sequence-like' but k-mer multiplicity is enormous.
